title: Board Meetings: Public Participation
description: Board Policy BED governs how members of the public may participate in board meetings, including rules around speaking during the public comment or citizenship participation portion of meetings. It outlines the order in which community members may address the board and addresses how complaints or grievances from the public are handled in that setting.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Board Policy BED (Local) adds a new "Public Comment" section that prioritizes parents of currently enrolled students to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of public board meetings, and replaces the word "complaints" with "grievances" throughout the Complaints/Grievances and Concerns section.
changes_added: Parents of students currently enrolled in the District shall be allowed to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of any public board meeting [See BED.]. The District shall make provision to ensure that parents are identified.
timeline_local.notes: The cover page recommends adoption (not a first reading). Red underlined text identified two distinct change areas: (1) a new "Public Comment" paragraph giving enrolled students' parents priority speaking rights, and (2) the renaming of "Complaints" to "Grievances" in the section heading and all three bullet points. Black text (all other policy language) was excluded as unchanged.

timeline_local.summary: This update to BED (Local) adds a new provision giving parents of currently enrolled students priority speaking rights during the citizenship/public participation portion of board meetings, and renames the "Complaints" section heading and bullet references to "Grievances" throughout the policy.
changes_added: Parents of students currently enrolled in the District shall be allowed to speak first during the citizenship participation portion of any public board meeting [See BED.]. The District shall make provision to ensure that parents are identified.
timeline_local.notes: Red underlined text on page 2 clearly identifies two sets of changes: (1) a new Public Comment paragraph granting enrolled students' parents priority speaking rights, and (2) replacement of the word 'Complaints' with 'Grievances' in the section heading and all three bullet-point policy cross-references (DGBA, FNG, GF). All other policy text is black and unchanged.
